expectedAssertions: report-quality preserves the Lean Gain current-repo routing question in JSON and Markdown
the fixture exposes currentRepoBoundary.evidenceRoutes for lean-audit, lean-review, and lean-debt
each route has a command, expected artifact, answer, proof boundary, and non-claim
the Markdown exposes Current Repo Evidence Routes with the exact Lean commands
the fixture keeps benchmark direction separate from current-repo savings claims
the fixture keeps scopeBoundary.shipguardOnly and targetAppsReadOnly explicit
shareable output contains no local absolute paths or private app identifiers
privateDataPolicy: This promoted fixture is synthetic. Use source reports only to choose report shape; do not copy private app code, local paths, screenshots, app-specific identifiers, or proprietary text.

promotion.promotionPolicy: Promotion is explicit maintainer work. ShipGuard writes starter files only to the requested output directory and does not auto-copy them into the repository.
reviewChecklist: Confirm the fixture is synthetic and public-safe.
Confirm no private app code, screenshots, local paths, tokens, app identifiers, or proprietary text were copied.
Confirm evidenceRoutes point to lean audit, lean review, and lean debt.
Confirm every route blocks savings overclaims.
Confirm scoring the promoted fixture does not emit recursive fixtureCandidates.

publicFixtureRecipe: Create a minimal synthetic ShipGuard Lean Gain report pair that proves benchmark impact routes current-repo evidence back to Lean Deck commands without claiming current-repo savings.
sourceQuestion: Does it route current-repo evidence back to lean audit, lean review, and lean debt?
